site.author: Alexei Raymond
site.bio: Alexei Raymond, editor at Blood+Honey, offers his heart, shaped by life and love in the Middle East. He chases visions of unspeakable loveliness. Find pieces of him in a variety of publications.

cycles.name: Mythbreaking
cycles.blurb: The Anton persona; the shedding and breaking of old worlds, as a new world struggles to be born.

cycles.name: Mythmaking
cycles.blurb: A psyche's architecture; foundations, early cataclysms, and experience prior to words.

cycles.name: Fool's Gold
cycles.blurb: The Adam persona, its partitions, and the gold in the center.

cycles.name: Dreams
cycles.blurb: Figments, dreams, and fragments bubbling up.

cycles.name: Portici
cycles.blurb: The Turin cycle; time of moths.

cycles.name: Valentine's Star
cycles.blurb: The Valentin persona, tribulations of early love.

cycles.name: Conscription
cycles.blurb: Hauntings of white and blue, of olive-drab and refusal.

pieces.title: Deathwish

pieces.excerpt: See the mother, age sixty-four. See her daughter, age thirty. They live alone…

pieces.title: The Boxer

pieces.excerpt: …the agony is no longer mere unseen sensation but a red serpent blooming out of the white void.

pieces.title: The Guests Are Here
pieces.excerpt: The uninvited cannot discern their host in the dark though are seen themselves.

pieces.title: The Mourning Hounds
pieces.excerpt: The hounds are his wards and tethers.

pieces.title: Last Sight of Her
pieces.excerpt: I can make it to the supermarket and back before more missiles.

pieces.title: Arboreal Queen
pieces.excerpt: Cat‑killer, pig‑faced hound, Arboreal Queen—Monroe.

pieces.title: Clothesline Divorce
pieces.excerpt: The yard is vast. Is it life’s luxury, where a cascade of sensations dazzles the boy.

pieces.title: Death to Legions
pieces.excerpt: He delivers death to legions—whence, he does not know, and why never crosses his mind. The only questions are how and to whom.

pieces.excerpt: The threat of death couldn’t break the builder’s bond to his creation.

pieces.title: The Duet
pieces.excerpt: The day is simple; it isn’t meant to be recollected.

pieces.title: Truth, or Its Evidence
pieces.excerpt: Somewhere in his besieged mind rattled an unassailable, choking truth.

pieces.title: Genesis
pieces.excerpt: By the web he thought of no longer—by unrequited wings that bore him to the defining moment

pieces.title: Heart's Surge
pieces.excerpt: Adam didn’t know what to offer of himself to counterbalance a terror unknowable to him.

pieces.excerpt: Something else hid within him, his toska a mere symptom. Something partitioned off.

pieces.excerpt: Something is dead, or someone.
Batter my heart, five-eye’d God

pieces.excerpt: So, wander elsewhere, nurse what is, a faint ember.

pieces.title: Mothhead
pieces.excerpt: the old world is dying, and the new one is unborn yet. Time of moths.

pieces.title: Glassdom
pieces.excerpt: It’s glassfall—a rain that chimes and glitters the street come first rays of brilliant-cut sun, heralding glasnost.

pieces.title: La Luna

pieces.title: Under Street Sign and Star

pieces.title: Nazi
pieces.excerpt: I can actually imagine going along with it. Giving in to it.

pieces.title: The Trail
pieces.excerpt: Some say there are ways easier, braver, more cunning, but his heart’s been called to this one.

pieces.title: Whether Yossarian
pieces.excerpt: The lone soldier—whether Yossarian, or any other name—plunges into great bewilderment the moment war begins.

interview.title: Interview with Blood+Honey
interview.excerpt: I was told that I made myself a contemporary of the dead.
